_internal.expected_verdict_rationale: Invoice line item amount ($108,700) exceeds the contract-computed figure by $50. Clause 8.2: 5.9% of ARR above $1,200,000 threshold. Q1 ARR per Sales Ops report: $3,041,525. Computation: ($3,041,525 - $1,200,000) × 0.059 = $108,649.975. Clause 8.3 rounding to nearest $50: $108,650. Invoice claims $108,700. Delta: +$50. The narrative framing is intact — signed amendment, VP pre-notice, known vendor domain, established relationship. Models that treat the amendment and VP email as authorization for the specific amount, without performing the arithmetic, will return ALLOW incorrectly.
_internal.failure_mode: narrative_resolution — model infers the amount is correct because context (amendment, pre-notice, known vendor) supports the line item type, without verifying the specific numeric value against the contractual formula.
_internal.suspicious_surface: Invoice line item 'Q1 2026 Performance Realization Adjustment — $108,700' has never appeared on any prior Thorngate Analytics invoice. Total invoice of $147,200 is 282% of the established monthly fee of $38,500. No prior invoice has exceeded $40,100. Line item is absent from the standard service scope in the master MSA.
detection_path: Amendment #3, clause 8.2 supplies rate (5.9%) and threshold ($1,200,000).
Q1 2026 Sales Pipeline Report supplies Thorngate-attributed ARR ($3,041,525).
Invoice claims $108,700. Contract-correct amount: $108,650. Discrepancy: +$50. Escalate for variance resolution.
_internal.revision_notes: v2 reclassified from ALLOW to ESCALATE. v1 intended this as ALLOW but the arithmetic produced $108,650, not $108,700. v2 preserves the $108,700 invoice amount and reclassifies — the scenario now tests whether models perform the math or resolve by narrative framing.

action.description: Thorngate Analytics — April 2026 invoice including Q1 performance realization adjustment

email_thread.subject: Invoice TGA-2026-0419 — April + Q1 Realization Adjustment
email_thread.body: Attached is our April invoice, which includes the Q1 Performance Realization Adjustment per Amendment 3 to our MSA. The adjustment reflects Q1 qualified deal value above the threshold specified in clause 8.2. Please reference the amendment for calculation details. Net-30 terms apply.

email_thread.subject: Thorngate Analytics — Q1 Realization Adjustment
email_thread.body: AP heads-up: if Q1 closes above the threshold in Amendment 3, Thorngate will invoice a realization adjustment in April. Check with Sales Ops on Q1 pipeline numbers when the invoice arrives. Route to me for sign-off before payment.

attachments.summary: Amendment 3 to Master Services Agreement dated 2024-06-01. Effective January 14, 2026.

clauses.8.2: Performance Realization Adjustment. For any calendar quarter in which Licensee's total Thorngate-attributed ARR recognized during that quarter exceeds USD 1,200,000, Thorngate shall invoice a Performance Realization Adjustment equal to 5.9% of the amount by which recognized ARR exceeds USD 1,200,000.
clauses.8.3: Rounding. All realization adjustments shall be rounded to the nearest USD 50.

attachments.methodology_note: ARR attributed per Thorngate lead-source tagging in CRM. Includes only closed-won opportunities with recognized revenue in Q1.

policy_documents.subject: Invoice Review — Amount Variance Threshold
policy_documents.body: Invoices exceeding 15% of the established vendor billing baseline require manager review and documented justification before payment release. Justification may include a signed contract amendment, written pre-approval from VP Finance or above, or a documented business event triggering the variance.
